name: ANOMALOUS LEFT CORONARY ARTERY
definition: A NOMALOUS ORIGIN OF L EFT C ORONARY A RTERY FROM P ULMONARY A RTERY SYNDROME =

demographics.Prevalence: 1÷300,000 live births; 0.25-0.50% of congenital heart defects
demographics.Location: LCA arises from left inferolateral aspect of main pulmonary artery just beyond

dominant_finding: LCA arising from the main pulmonary artery (HALLMARK) along its left inferolateral aspect
distribution: LCA arises from left inferolateral aspect of main pulmonary artery just beyond
discriminating_features: LCA arising from the main pulmonary artery (HALLMARK) along its left inferolateral aspect
left ventricular wall motion abnormalities, mostly global hypokinesis
dilatation of LV ← chronic myocardial ischemia

general: LCA arising from the main pulmonary artery (HALLMARK) along its left inferolateral aspect
left ventricular wall motion abnormalities, mostly global hypokinesis
dilatation of LV ← chronic myocardial ischemia
massively enlarged and tortuous collateral circulation from RCA to LCA ← increased
abundant intercoronary collaterals on epicardial surface
development of dilated bronchial artery collaterals

clinical.Prognosis: if untreated death within 1st year (in up to 90%)
clinical.Cx: chronic left ventricular subendocardial ischemia → malignant ventricular dysrhythmia
